title: RestoPlatform
summary: All-in-one software for independent restaurants: QR ordering, pay-at-table, reservations, delivery and a website of their own, with zero commission on what they sell.

caseStudy.sector: SaaS · Restaurants, hosted entirely in Europe
caseStudy.role: Sole architect-developer, supported by an AI agent workflow
caseStudy.timeline: Designed and built in a few months, first restaurant now live
caseStudy.challenge: An independent restaurant owner juggles a dozen tools, menu, reservations, QR ordering, payment, delivery, analytics, and still hands a slice of every meal to delivery aggregators. RestoPlatform replaces the lot with a single platform: one website per restaurant, and not a cent of commission on what they sell.
caseStudy.approach: One platform, three uses: a public website for each restaurant (built to rank on Google), a smooth ordering experience for guests, and a back-office where staff run their service. Each restaurant's data is fully walled off from the others, and everything is hosted in Europe. Under the hood, a single .NET codebase serves all three.
caseStudy.outcome: The first restaurant is live. Owners get one tool instead of ten, keep every euro of every bill (a flat subscription replaces per-meal commissions), and own a website that ranks. Built privacy-first, hosted entirely in Europe, and backed by a deep automated test suite so new features ship without breaking what already works.
caseStudy.diagramAlt: RestoPlatform Clean Architecture: REST API + SignalR, a Blazor SSR showcase site, a Blazor Server dashboard, a multi-tenant application layer, EF Core 10 / PostgreSQL infrastructure with Row-Level Security on Clever Cloud

decisions.title: Every restaurant's data stays walled off
decisions.detail: All restaurants share one database, but each one only ever sees its own data, enforced at several independent layers, right down to the database engine itself. Try to reach another restaurant's data and you simply get a "not found". Every type of data is tested for this before it ships.

decisions.title: Your data never leaves Europe
decisions.detail: No US hyperscaler: everything runs on a French, sovereignty-certified host. Costs stay predictable for a flat-subscription model, and customer data stays in Europe end to end.

decisions.title: Zero commission, just a flat fee
decisions.detail: The platform never takes a cut of a meal; card and delivery fees are passed straight through. The tool is aligned with the owner's takings, not with skimming margin, the structural difference versus the aggregators.

decisions.title: Allergen warnings can never be hidden
decisions.detail: As long as a dish can be ordered, its allergen warning stays on screen, impossible to hide or style away. On food, that's a safety rule built into the product, not a setting.

decisions.title: Restaurant sites built to rank on Google
decisions.detail: Each restaurant's public site is server-rendered with proper structured data and language tags, so it shows up in search and brings in covers directly, with no dependence on a marketplace.

decisions.title: Each restaurant gets paid directly
decisions.detail: Every restaurant keeps its own payment account, its own payouts and its own dispute handling. Card details never pass through us, which keeps the platform at the lightest compliance tier.

sections.title: How restaurants stay separate
body: All restaurants live in one shared database, but a restaurant only ever sees its own data. That separation is enforced at four independent layers, the last one inside the database engine itself, so a single mistake in code can't leak one restaurant's data to another.
It's not left to trust: every restaurant-owned type of data has an automated test proving it can't be reached from another account, run against a real database before any change ships.

sections.title: Payment & privacy
body: Each restaurant is the merchant and gets paid directly; card data never touches the platform. Tax is applied correctly per channel (on-site, takeaway, delivery), and prices can display in many currencies while the euro stays the only one actually charged.
Privacy is built in: data stays in Europe, every change is logged in a separate tamper-proof history, and guests can access, export or erase their data on request.

sections.title: Live updates for staff
body: The kitchen pass and waiter calls update in real time, and the staff app gets push notifications, so the floor and the kitchen always see the same picture.
Background jobs (reminders, housekeeping) run reliably without a heavy messaging system, kept deliberately simple for this stage.

sections.title: Quality that protects the launch
body: The product is backed by a large automated test suite, with more test code than product code, including checks against a real database so data stays correctly separated.
Nothing reaches the live branch without a green build and passing tests; the main line stays under human review.

title: DPELib
summary: A platform that connects the five players around France's energy-performance certificate, homeowners, diagnosticians, notaries, agencies and tradespeople, anonymously, with secure payment and full data-protection compliance.

caseStudy.role: Sole architect-developer, with AI agents to speed up delivery
caseStudy.challenge: Bring together five groups who don't naturally trust each other, homeowners, certified diagnosticians, notaries, estate agencies and tradespeople, on one platform. With identities kept private by default, payment that's safe for both sides, and a stack of French regulations to satisfy from day one.
caseStudy.approach: A single platform with four tailored experiences: a mobile app for homeowners, a dashboard for professionals, a public showcase built for search, and the engine behind them. A diagnostician stays anonymous until a job is confirmed, the client's payment is only taken once the appointment is locked in, and the public directory of professionals refreshes itself nightly from open government data. One .NET codebase powers it all.
caseStudy.outcome: A genuine trusted middleman: the homeowner books and pays safely, the professional stays anonymous until they accept, and money only changes hands when the appointment is actually scheduled, otherwise it's released automatically and the client can re-book in one click. Fully compliant with France's data-protection and payment rules, and deployed with no secrets left lying around.
caseStudy.diagramAlt: DPELib Clean Architecture: 4 presentation apps, 153 application use cases, EF Core 10 / SQL Server infrastructure with a separate audit database

decisions.title: Anonymous until a job is confirmed
decisions.detail: A diagnostician's identity stays hidden until the appointment is confirmed; even phone calls go through a masking layer until then. On a matchmaking platform, trust is earned step by step, it isn't presumed.

decisions.title: Pay only when the appointment is locked in
decisions.detail: Money is reserved when the client books but only taken once the professional confirms. If they go quiet, automatic reminders go out, and after a week the hold is released. Nobody pays for an appointment that doesn't happen.

decisions.title: Useful from day one
decisions.detail: The official public register is imported every night, so every diagnostician already has a profile, no account needed, created only when a client reaches out. The platform is helpful immediately, without waiting for sign-ups.

decisions.title: Privacy guaranteed by design
decisions.detail: Consents and history live in a separate, append-only store, and nothing is ever truly deleted, just anonymised. The right to erasure and full traceability are guaranteed by how it's built, not by remembering to do it.

decisions.title: Reliability built into the build
decisions.detail: An automated check makes sure every feature is correctly wired up before the app can even build, so adding capability stays safe rather than nerve-racking across 150+ features.

decisions.title: Deployed with zero stored secrets
decisions.detail: The release pipeline holds no long-lived passwords or keys, it's replayable, auditable, and leaves nothing sensitive lying around.

sections.title: From booking to payment
body: The client picks a slot and their card is pre-authorised (€80 + €30 per extra item) with strong, bank-grade verification. The professional is alerted by email and text.

Confirm, and the payment goes through, the identity is revealed and the appointment is set. Stay silent, and reminders go out automatically, then after a week the hold is released and the client is offered a one-click re-booking.

sections.title: Connected to the right systems
body: Six outside services are plugged in, payment, the official open-data register, on-device image recognition, a building-data source, phone-number masking and avatars, each kept cleanly separate and replaceable.
That isolation means any one of them can change or be swapped out without touching the rest of the platform.

sections.title: Built to run reliably
body: Every release goes through an automated pipeline, build, full test run, coverage check, then deploy and a post-release health check, so a broken version never reaches users.
The whole hosting setup is defined as code, runs on demand to keep costs down, and keeps its secrets in a secure vault.

sections.title: Regulatory compliance
body: Granular consent, a separate consent log, and the right to erasure within 30 days. A blocking consent banner before any non-essential tracker runs.
Bank-grade payment authentication, the official energy-certificate calculation method, and read-only alerts on properties that can no longer be rented out.

title: Brasa Geneva
summary: A complete platform for a Geneva cigar club: an adults-only public showcase, a member area, and a secure admin that runs the whole business, catalogue, subscriptions, payments and an art gallery.
imageAlt: Brasa Geneva logo engraved in brushed brass

caseStudy.sector: Luxury · regulated e-commerce, premium cigars, humidors and an art gallery for a private Geneva club
caseStudy.role: Sole architect-developer running a multi-agent AI workflow
caseStudy.challenge: A private Geneva club wanted one platform to sell premium cigars, artisan humidors and an art gallery, public showcase, member area and back-office in one, inside one of the strictest legal frames there is: tobacco advertising rules, a ban on cross-border sales, mandatory age checks, data protection and strong payment security.
caseStudy.approach: One platform with three faces, an age-gated public showcase, a member area, and a back-office that runs the business, with the legal rules built into the product rather than bolted on. Hosted entirely in Switzerland, three ways to pay, and a separate audit trail for compliance. One .NET codebase behind all of it.
caseStudy.outcome: A luxury storefront that's compliant by design: cigars can't be bought outside Switzerland, the age check can't be skipped, and every change is logged in a separate audit trail. Delivered in around four and a half months, with more effort spent on automated testing than on the product code itself, so the club launched on solid ground.
caseStudy.diagramAlt: Brasa Geneva Clean Architecture: 3 presentation apps, 99 application use cases, EF Core 10 / PostgreSQL infrastructure with a separate audit database

decisions.title: The law is built into the product
decisions.detail: Selling cigars outside Switzerland is blocked and the age check can't be skipped, both enforced as core rules, impossible to bypass with a clever URL. Here a bypass isn't a bug, it's a legal risk.

decisions.title: A separate, tamper-proof audit trail
decisions.detail: Every change is recorded in an append-only history kept apart from the live data. Compliance doesn't rely on good intentions in the code, it's structural.

decisions.title: Hosted entirely in Switzerland
decisions.detail: No US or global cloud: keeping the data in Switzerland matches the Geneva luxury positioning, and the hosting stays under full control end to end.

decisions.title: One secured door to the data
decisions.detail: The back-office reaches the data only through the same internal service everything else uses, so there's a single surface to secure and audit, not several.

decisions.title: Kept deliberately simple
decisions.detail: Background tasks (loyalty badges, restock reminders) run on a lightweight scheduler. Heavier infrastructure is scoped but left switched off until there's a real need, no complexity paid for in advance.

decisions.title: Quality first
decisions.detail: Every feature is required to have its own tests before it can ship, so an untested rule can't slip into a regulated business. Quality is what protects the client.

sections.title: Three ways to pay
body: One checkout, three options, card, bank transfer and crypto, with card payments handled by a trusted provider.
The legal guardrail lives inside checkout: cigars can only be bought in Switzerland; from elsewhere the journey switches to a quote or Club-only access.

sections.title: Hosting & operations
body: The whole platform runs on Swiss infrastructure, fully defined as code so it can be rebuilt or scaled reliably.
A green build and passing tests are required before any change is committed.

sections.title: Regulatory compliance
body: Tobacco-advertising rules respected, cross-border sales blocked, an age check that can't be skipped.
Data protection by design: a separate audit trail, nothing hard-deleted, no personal data in plain text in the logs. The art gallery is protected against casual copying.

sections.title: Languages & "light luxury" design
body: Seven languages, every visible word professionally translated.
A parchment, ink and ember aesthetic, slow, restrained animations and a careful, hand-finished feel throughout.
